purpose: The commit adds a check in hci_cmd_sync_queue to return an error when the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set, preventing commands from being queued after the device has been unregistered, which would otherwise lead to a use‑after‑free crash.

The commit removes an unnecessary inclusion of asm/termios.h from the file n_hdlc.c because the required termios definitions are already provided via linux/tty.h, which pulls linux/termios.h. This cleans up the include hierarchy and eliminates a stray header.
The commit updates the perf c2c documentation to include the newly added 'N/A' metric for store operations, reflecting changes in the report table.

The patch fixes race conditions and double‑free bugs in the hugetlb VMA lock handling, ensures a VMA lock is allocated for every VM_MAYSHARE VMA during split, changes unmap handling to free the lock instead of clearing VM_MAYSHARE, and updates sharing logic to make the lock a prerequisite for PMD sharing.

The commit updates the device‑tree binding documentation for the Realtek rt5682s codec, adding entries for the AVDD and MICVDD power supplies that are already used in a specific board DTS. No functional code is changed; only the binding .yaml (or .txt) is amended to reflect existing hardware requirements.

The commit adds a missing iounmap() call in the driver’s remove routine for the zorro7xx SCSI driver, correcting a resource leak that occurs when the probe’s error handling allocated I/O memory that was never released on removal.
The commit deletes an unused 'work' member from the struct mlx5e_async_ctx in the mlx5e driver (kTLS path), cleaning up dead code and simplifying the data structure.

The commit changes the type of cfg80211_auth_request::key_idx from unsigned to signed so that the code can safely assign -1 (used to indicate "no key") without triggering static analysis warnings. No functional behavior is altered; the change is purely to improve code correctness and tooling compatibility.

The commit reorders the entries of CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in the RISC‑V defconfig files so that they appear in the same sorted order as savedefconfig generates, without altering any functional behavior.

The commit adds a new helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to the MCP251xfd CAN driver and replaces existing inline checks for FD mode with calls to this function, improving code readability and maintainability.

The commit modifies the RTC self‑test suite to detect whether an RTC device is present on the platform. If no RTC is found, the test is skipped instead of being marked as failed, preventing unnecessary test failures on systems without RTC hardware.

The commit removes a dead store of the variable 'bytes' in the ALSA echoaudio driver. The initial assignment is never read because the variable is overwritten later, so the change deletes the redundant line, silencing a clang static‑analysis warning.

The commit modifies the rcuscale configuration so that building rcuscale no longer forces the inclusion of RCU Tasks on CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE kernels. This removes an unnecessary dependency, reducing kernel size and simplifying tracing.
The commit corrects a duplicated phrase "be split be split" to a single "be split" in the spte.h header of the KVM x86 MMU code, fixing a typo in a comment without changing functionality.

The commit reverts a previous modification that populated the DCN315 clock table using DCFCLK values. Because the power management firmware does not fill the table when DCFCLK states are disabled, using those values would cause a read underflow. The revert restores the earlier behavior to avoid this underflow until a proper solution is implemented.

The commit updates the device tree bindings documentation for the Mali Bifrost GPU to include a compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L (R9A07G054) SoC, enabling proper description of this hardware in device trees.

The commit renames the structure `pcie_port` to `dw_pcie_rp` throughout the DesignWare PCIe core driver to align with the naming convention that prefixes driver‑specific entities with "dw_" and suffixes endpoint‑related ones with "_ep". No functional logic is altered.

The commit updates the format string used in a netdev_dbg call within the amd-xgbe driver. It replaces the "%#06hx" specifier (which expects an unsigned short) with a specifier that matches the promoted int type of the argument (e.g., "%#06x"), thereby eliminating a clang -Wformat warning and aligning with kernel printk format guidelines.

The commit adjusts the branch range check in the MIPS jump_label implementation by casting the upper bound to a signed long before comparison, ensuring that negative offsets are not mistakenly considered within range and thus eliminating spurious warnings.

The commit removes the HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case from the SetHwReg8188EU() function in the r8188eu driver, because that case only called another function. The function is now called directly, eliminating an unnecessary abstraction layer.

The commit adds the 'static' keyword to symbols in arch/arm64/kernel/cpu_errata.c that are not meant to be exported globally. This silences sparse warnings about undeclared symbols and limits the symbols' linkage to the file, improving code hygiene.
The commit updates the MSM display device tree bindings by removing an interrupt‑flags property from MDSS nodes because the interrupt provider supplies a single cell, making the flags cell invalid.

The commit adds allocation for the array members inside struct ctrl_ref in the v4l2-ctrls subsystem, enabling drivers to later change the array size. It modifies the allocation routine to reserve space for these arrays.

The commit extends the socinfo driver by adding entries for additional Qualcomm SoCs (SM8350, SC8280XP, SM8450) and PMICs (including SA8540P). This enables the kernel to recognize these platforms, which were previously missing.

The commit expands the rtl8xxxu Wi‑Fi driver’s chip‑cut detection to recognise all possible version letters (A‑P) instead of treating anything beyond 'E' as unknown, updating the relevant tables/logic accordingly.
The commit removes the .is_enabled field from the PRCMU clock driver for ux500 platforms because the generic clock core already maintains a software enable count, making the custom count redundant.

The commit refactors RISC‑V memory‑management initialization code to reduce the number of #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL directives. It groups XIP‑specific macro definitions with the variables they affect and moves the pt_ops macro next to the related #ifdef, consolidating the three identified patterns of XIP usage.

The commit updates the arm64 Qualcomm sm6350-lena device tree to enable QUP and GPI DMA controllers. This change makes the DMA hardware available so that functional I2C nodes can be added in future work.

The commit updates comments in the rzg2l thermal driver: replaces an unclear comment with a clearer one, corrects a typo (caliberation → calibration), and moves the opening /* to its own line to follow coding style. No functional code changes are made.

The commit modifies the igc driver’s release_swfw_sync routine to detect failure when acquiring the hardware semaphore, logs an error, and exits the loop, preventing an infinite loop that occurs when the hardware is surprise‑removed.
The commit updates the pcm3168a ASoC driver to replace old clock‑related defines with the newer ASoC DAI terminology, aligning the code with current kernel conventions.
The commit deletes a conditional check for IS_SWAPFILE in the NFS code because the macro is always defined, eliminating dead code.

The commit adds handling for GTP_ECHO_REQ messages in the kernel GTP driver. When such a packet arrives, the code now constructs and sends a GTP_ECHO_RSP containing the GTPIE_RECOVERY information element with the appropriate restart counter (0 for GTPv1, user‑provided for GTPv0). This implements the echo response required by the GTP specification, which was previously missing when a GTP device was created via ip link.

The commit replaces uses of cpumask_next_* functions with the more efficient cpumask_first_* equivalents when the start index is -1 (i.e., start == 0). It also introduces a new helper function cpumask_first_zero() because no such function existed. The change is aimed at improving efficiency and simplifying trivial cases.

The commit removes the driver’s attempt to query the firmware for TIM information via the "dtim_assoc" IOVAR, which is no longer supported. This stops the repeated error message "wl dtim_assoc failed (-52)" that appears after a station roams between access points.

Adds a new board configuration (adl_rt5682) to support the alc5682 headset codec on Intel platforms that do not have a speaker amplifier, wiring the codec to SSP0 following the Intel Bluetooth offload design.

The commit adds a validation in the io_uring networking code so that zerocopy sendmsg requests are rejected when the underlying socket does not support zerocopy, aligning the behavior with the existing check for zerocopy send.

The commit updates the kerneldoc preamble script to check for the presence of xeCJK.sty before attempting to load it. This avoids a XeLaTeX error on systems where the texlive-xecjk package is not installed, allowing "make pdfdocs" to succeed for non‑CJK documentation.

The commit modifies the checkpatch.pl script to recognize macros that expand to a 'case' statement (e.g., #define C2S(x) case x: return #x) and suppresses the warning "Macros with complex values should be enclosed in parentheses" for such macros, because they are legitimately used in many drivers.

The commit replaces the simple‑KMS helper functions in the simpledrm driver with the standard DRM atomic helper functions, removes empty CRTC atomic enable/disable helpers, and unconditionally calls drm_atomic_helper_check_plane_state(). No functional changes are introduced; the change is purely architectural refactoring.
The commit introduces a JPEG decoding worker interface in the MediaTek JPEG decoder driver, enabling three hardware decoding units to operate in parallel on the MT8195 platform.

The commit removes several VPRINTK() debug print calls from the libata driver because the same information is already emitted via tracepoints, and also deletes the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() helper which is redundant since SCSI tracing provides the same data. No functional code is changed, only cleanup of unnecessary debugging output.

The commit adds support for entering the GPU's low‑power "zstate" during the extra vertical blank period that appears when FreeSync video mode is active. It triggers a full display update, passes a modified vtotal with extra lines to the display manager, computes the extra time in microseconds, and enables zstate entry if the extra time exceeds 5 ms. It also computes an optimized min_dst_y_next_start value and restores defaults when leaving FreeSync.

The commit changes the prototype of the hqd_destroy() callback in the AMDGPU KFD driver so that its third parameter, reset_type, is declared as uint32_t instead of enum kfd_preempt_type, matching the actual implementations and eliminating a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) type mismatch that caused a kernel CFI failure on systems built with CONFIG_CFI_CLANG.

The commit updates the device‑tree binding for STM32 pinctrl: it renames the property from 'reset' to the correct 'resets' and adds an 'additionalProperties: false' clause to the schema, fixing the documentation error and tightening validation.

The commit modifies the ath9k driver to silence an array‑bounds warning emitted by GCC 12 in the function ath9k_hw_resettxqueue, likely by adding a pragma or comment that lowers the warning level for that line.

The commit modifies the sun8i-ss crypto driver to replace calls to pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get, which is a more suitable runtime power management API and simplifies the code flow.

The commit reorganizes io_uring code by moving the fixed file installation/removal helpers into the filetable area and changes their signatures to accept a context (ctx) instead of an io_kiocb, because the operation is not tied to a specific request.

The commit updates the device‑tree binding documentation for the NXP i.MX8MQ VPU, splitting the previously combined G1 and G2 decoder blocks into separate nodes because they are independent and controlled by the vpu‑blk‑ctrl power‑domain.

The commit removes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro from the iwlwifi driver configuration code and inlines its single use directly into the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro, simplifying the macro definitions without altering functionality.
